sourceSpec.title: AEA Architecture Figure — Master Specification
notes: Detailed per-arrow sections override conflicting summary prose.
The app mirrors the spec as structured graph data and keeps semantic objects spec-locked.
March 2026 review reconciled the master spec with the runtime graph and refreshed standard version metadata.

aliases.rationale: Legacy alias retained for backwards compatibility; the canonical block id is DEC_G2.

C1.label: Agent remains in the NOA M+O domain
C1.summary: All autonomous agent logic resides in psM+O and never inside CPC.

C2.label: Sensing is read-only through a diode
C2.summary: The CPC-to-M+O sensing path is unidirectional through the NE 177 read chain.

C3.label: Decision-making is semantically grounded and gated
C3.summary: PA-DIM, AAS, retrieval controls, context guards, schema enforcement, policy guardrails, deterministic validators, and human approval constrain decisions before VoR emission.

C4.label: Actuation is exclusive to the VoR path
C4.summary: The only write-back initiation path is F5 through NE 178 and the VoR Interface.

C5.label: Telemetry is publish-only and decoupled
C5.summary: KPI publication flows northbound independently from actuation.

C6.label: AI safety controls are deterministic and external to the model
C6.summary: Input classification, retrieval access control, tool brokering, output schema enforcement, approval freshness, and monitoring execute as independent deterministic controls rather than prompt-only instructions.

nodes.title: Lane A
nodes.subtitle: Core Process Control (CPC / OT)
nodes.description: CPC lane holding the DCS or PLC system, the read-only data interface, and the change handler that receives VoR-approved write instructions.

inspector.rationale: Lane A visualizes the protected CPC zone that remains independent from agent execution logic.
notes: Lane A never hosts the autonomous agent.
Write-back terminates at the CPC Change Handler, not at the CPC Data Interface.

nodes.title: Lane B
nodes.subtitle: Plant-specific M+O (psM+O edge)
nodes.description: Plant-specific Monitoring and Optimization lane containing the AEA, gateway-adjacent consumption, decision logic, and VoR request composition.

inspector.rationale: Lane B is the only place where agent logic executes, satisfying claim C1 and preserving CPC isolation.
notes: Sense, Decide, and Act are logical bands, not separate security zones.
The VoR request originates here and is handed to the NE 178 interface.

nodes.title: Lane C
nodes.subtitle: Central M+O (off-prem)
nodes.description: Central analytics and historian lane consuming publish-only KPI telemetry over MQTT.

inspector.rationale: Lane C is intentionally decoupled from actuation and only receives publish-only telemetry.
notes: No write-back edge returns from Lane C into CPC or psM+O.
Optional F7_sub shows subscription semantics without changing the one-way KPI stream.

nodes.title: NOA Security Gateway
nodes.subtitle: NE 177 / NE 178
nodes.description: Boundary container that separates CPC from psM+O. The upper section is the NE 177 read-only chain and the lower section is the NE 178 VoR interface.

inspector.rationale: The gateway enforces separate sensing and actuation channels so the data diode is never conflated with write-back.
notes: The upper section is NE 177 and must never imply a feedback path.
The lower section is the separate NE 178 VoR interface, not a fourth NE 177 module.

nodes.title: Autonomous Edge Agent (AEA)
nodes.subtitle: psM+O edge node
nodes.description: Large container grouping the Sense, Decide, and Act bands on the plant-specific M+O edge node.

inspector.rationale: The AEA groups all agent-side processing while keeping the publication architecture aligned to the Sense–Decide–Act narrative.
notes: The AEA is a logical container, not an additional security boundary.
Nodes inside the AEA are positioned within fixed board slots inside each band.

nodes.title: Sense
nodes.subtitle: Data acquisition and normalisation
nodes.description: Sense band for OPC UA ingestion, PA-DIM normalization, and the canonical t0 telemetry snapshot.

inspector.rationale: The Sense band holds the read-only telemetry ingress and freezes the t0 snapshot consumed downstream.
notes: F3d and F3h both source from the same time-stamped snapshot.
The band remains entirely on the psM+O side of the gateway.

nodes.title: Decide
nodes.subtitle: Reasoning, retrieval, and gating
nodes.description: Decide band for semantic retrieval, LLM planning, policy enforcement, and deterministic validation.

inspector.rationale: This band encodes the semantically grounded decision pipeline and the mandatory guard/validator stages before actuation.
notes: Soft policy grounding and hard policy enforcement are intentionally separate flows.
Tool-call edges are dotted so they cannot be confused with telemetry or status.

nodes.title: Act
nodes.subtitle: Validated actuation and KPI publication
nodes.description: Act band for VoR request composition, publish-only KPI emission, and audit records.

inspector.rationale: The Act band is where validated plans bifurcate into the exclusive VoR write path and the decoupled KPI publication path.
notes: KPI publication is driven from harmonized signals, not from actuation outcomes.
Audit capture is separated from northbound telemetry.

nodes.title: CPC System
nodes.subtitle: DCS / PLC
nodes.description: Core process control system that remains SIL-rated and independent from the M+O agent.

inspector.rationale: The CPC System stays autonomous from agent logic even when the change handler executes approved instructions.
notes: The optional internal CPC arrow F_CPC_INT terminates here if rendered.

nodes.title: CPC Data Interface
nodes.subtitle: OPC UA Server · PA-DIM information model
nodes.description: Read-only sensing endpoint exposing CPC telemetry in the PA-DIM model to the security gateway.

inspector.rationale: A2 is the source for all telemetry and must remain read-only from the perspective of the gateway and the AEA.
notes: Nothing writes through A2. The exclusive write target is A3.

nodes.title: CPC Change Handler
nodes.subtitle: Executes or queues VoR-accepted changes
nodes.description: Exclusive write target for VoR-approved mapped change instructions.

inspector.rationale: A3, not A2, is the exclusive CPC-side target for validated change instructions.
notes: VoR write-back terminates here before any optional internal CPC execution arrow.

nodes.title: G1
nodes.subtitle: Data Retrieval
nodes.description: CPC-side retrieval module that subscribes to the CPC Data Interface.

inspector.rationale: G1 is the CPC-side subscription endpoint in the read-only chain.

nodes.title: G2
nodes.subtitle: Unidirectional Transfer / Data Diode
nodes.description: One-way transfer module enforcing the absence of any feedback path from psM+O to CPC for sensing.

inspector.rationale: G2 is the hardware or logical data diode that makes the read-only sensing guarantee explicit.
notes: The 'unidirectional — no feedback path' guarantee is the core of claim C2.

nodes.title: G3
nodes.subtitle: Data Provision
nodes.description: psM+O-side provision module exposing a mirrored PA-DIM endpoint to the AEA.

inspector.rationale: G3 mirrors the PA-DIM telemetry interface into psM+O without exposing a return channel.

nodes.title: VoR Interface
nodes.subtitle: NE 178 interface
nodes.description: Separate security function handling authentication, authorisation, semantic validation, mapping, audit event emission, and non-plant-specific status feedback for write-back.

inspector.rationale: VOI is distinct from the NE 177 read-only chain and is the only CPC boundary service allowed to receive F5 write requests.
notes: VOI enforces authentication, authorisation, semantic validation, mapping, and emits audit events for ACT3 to persist.
Status feedback is intentionally non-plant-specific.

nodes.title: OPC UA Client
nodes.subtitle: Read-only session · subscribes to PA-DIM nodes
nodes.description: AEA-side client consuming the mirrored PA-DIM endpoint through a read-only session role.

inspector.rationale: S1 keeps the sensing path read-only and exposes an explicit on-demand tool endpoint via opcua.read.
notes: The OPC UA session role is provisioned by the gateway/server side, not self-assigned by the client.

nodes.title: PA-DIM Harmonizer
nodes.subtitle: Normalises signals; assigns IEC 61987 IRDIs; time-stamps feature vector (t0)
nodes.description: Normalization block converting heterogeneous device signals into a harmonized PA-DIM-aligned snapshot with shared t0 semantics.

inspector.rationale: S2 creates the frozen t0 snapshot that must be shared between reasoning and deterministic validation.
notes: F3d and F3h must source from the same snapshot.
KPI inputs also originate here to keep telemetry independent from actuation.

nodes.title: AAS Repository
nodes.subtitle: Submodel templates; device configurations; operational constraints
nodes.description: Primary semantic context source containing typed AAS submodels, units, bounds, and operational constraints.

inspector.rationale: AAS submodels provide the typed context, bounds, and mapping data needed for grounded reasoning and deterministic validation.

nodes.title: Policy Store
nodes.subtitle: Site-specific operational policies; safety interlocks; change approval rules
nodes.description: Policy source with separate retrieval, refusal, and hard-enforcement outbound paths.

inspector.rationale: The policy store supplies soft retrieval grounding, refusal criteria, and a separate hard-enforcement feed for deterministic policy validation.
notes: F3b and F3b' must never be collapsed into a single arrow.
Refusal and sensitive-topic rules are external policy, not prompt text.

nodes.title: Retrieval Guard
nodes.subtitle: ACL-aware; provenance-tagged; source-scoped retrieval filter
nodes.description: Deterministic access-control gate between the semantic stores and the retrieval stage.

inspector.rationale: DEC_R0 enforces ACL scope, provenance, and source trust before repository material can reach the RAG stage.
notes: Retrieved chunks must carry source ID, timestamp, scope, and trust classification.
Only approved slices may reach DEC_R1.

nodes.title: RAG / Knowledge Retrieval
nodes.subtitle: AAS-structured retrieval; semantic chunking; IDTA Part 2 API
nodes.description: Retrieval stage collecting AAS excerpts and policy summaries for the guarded planner context.

inspector.rationale: DEC_R1 assembles approved AAS and policy context before the input/context guard classifies it for model use.

nodes.title: Input / Context Guard
nodes.subtitle: Sole planner ingress; classifies, redacts, and refuses before model access
nodes.description: Deterministic input gate for retrieved text, plant snapshots, and tool observations before any model sees them.

inspector.rationale: DEC_G0 is the sole ingress into the planner context window and enforces classification, redaction, refusal, and snapshot consistency before model access.
notes: Retrieved narrative, plant-state snapshots, and tool observations all terminate here first.
Refusal policy is sourced from DEC_K2, not from prompt text.

nodes.title: LLM Agent / Planner
nodes.subtitle: Tool-using; ReAct reasoning loop
nodes.description: Planner that consumes only guarded context, proposes structured plans, and reacts to deterministic schema, policy, validator, or human rejections.

inspector.rationale: The planner operates inside psM+O, but all inbound context and outbound tool use are mediated by deterministic controls before any actuation path is available.
notes: Local tools: anomaly.detect, kpi.compute, sim.check.
External arrows only exist for brokered opcua.read and aas.query.

nodes.title: Tool Guard / Broker
nodes.subtitle: Read-only tool mediation; schema-checked requests; guarded observations
nodes.description: Deterministic broker that is solely responsible for dispatching external tool calls and returning observations through the guarded context path.

inspector.rationale: DEC_T0 enforces tool allowlists, parameter schemas, read-only guarantees, rate limits, provenance tagging, and monitored re-entry of tool observations.
notes: DEC_R2 no longer invokes external tools directly.
Tool observations must return through DEC_G0 before they can affect reasoning.

nodes.title: Output Guard / Schema Checker
nodes.subtitle: Strict plan schema; deterministic fields remain external
nodes.description: Schema-validation gate that accepts only a machine-readable plan object and rejects free text or unsupported fields before policy evaluation.

inspector.rationale: DEC_G1A rejects any non-structured or unsupported model output before deterministic policy, validation, or approval logic runs.
notes: Required fields are structural only: action_type, asset_id, parameter_id, requested_value, unit, snapshot_id, evidence_refs.
Risk tier, approval requirement, and approval token are deterministic downstream outputs, not LLM-authored fields.

nodes.title: Policy-Guard
nodes.subtitle: Runtime policy enforcement; rejects non-compliant proposals
nodes.description: Hard policy enforcement stage that consumes direct policy rules and emits either compliant candidates or rejection feedback.

inspector.rationale: DEC_G1 is the non-bypassable policy guard that receives hard rules from DEC_K2 and forces the planner loop to react to rejections.

nodes.title: Deterministic Validators
nodes.subtitle: AAS bounds validator + OPC UA RBAC validator
nodes.description: Deterministic stage validating typed bounds, current values, permitted write scope, plan hash, and approval freshness before any VoR request is composed, with explicit rejection feedback to the planner on failure.

inspector.rationale: DEC_G2 is the final deterministic barrier before human approval and VoR request composition and therefore carries both AAS and RBAC evidence.
notes: Legacy alias DEC3 is retained for backwards compatibility.
Approval freshness is bound to plan_hash + snapshot_id + expires_at.
Validator failures return machine-readable feedback to DEC_R2 via F_G2_reject.

nodes.title: Human Approval Gate
nodes.subtitle: All VoR-bound plans require explicit operator approval
nodes.description: Human-in-the-loop gate that approves a specific plan hash against a specific snapshot and expiry window before VoR composition.

inspector.rationale: DEC_H1 makes human approval mandatory for all VoR-bound plans and binds approval to the validated plan hash, snapshot, and expiry window.
notes: If an operator rejects the plan, it returns to DEC_R2 for replanning.
If approval expires or t0 changes before ACT1, the plan returns to DEC_G2 for deterministic revalidation.
Panel B remains an NE 178 protocol view and does not absorb this AEA-side gate.

nodes.title: Guardrail Monitor / Evals
nodes.subtitle: Write-once event stream for guardrails, denials, and approvals
nodes.description: Sidecar monitor that aggregates guardrail pass, reject, deny, and approval events for audit and evaluation.

inspector.rationale: DEC_M1 makes the guardrail layer observable, reviewable, and operationally testable without becoming an actuation path.
notes: Monitoring is a sidecar and must not block decision execution.
Events are write-once and immutable once committed.

nodes.title: VoR Request Composer
nodes.subtitle: Primary actuation path; composes non-plant-specific VoR request payload
nodes.description: AEA-side actuation block that translates the approved plan into the non-plant-specific VoR request payload.

inspector.rationale: ACT1 owns the exclusive write-back initiation path and can only be entered through explicit approval or feedback from the VoR interface.

nodes.title: KPI Publisher
nodes.subtitle: Publish-only; OPC UA PubSub over MQTT
nodes.description: Publish-only northbound telemetry publisher decoupled from actuation.

inspector.rationale: ACT2 proves that KPI publication is publish-only and decoupled from the VoR path.

nodes.title: Audit Log
nodes.subtitle: Decision events; VoR request records; guardrail outcomes
nodes.description: Dedicated audit sink for decision, guardrail, and VoR records, separated from KPI publication.

inspector.rationale: Audit records are separated from KPI telemetry so the actuation trail and guardrail trail remain explicit and reviewable.

nodes.title: MQTT Broker
nodes.subtitle: MQTT v5.0
nodes.description: Central messaging broker receiving publish-only KPI messages from the AEA.

inspector.rationale: The broker receives KPI publication only and never feeds a control signal back into psM+O or CPC.

nodes.title: Central Analytics · Historians · Dashboards
nodes.subtitle: Subscribe / consume
nodes.description: Central M+O consumers subscribing to KPI streams for analytics, historian ingestion, and dashboarding.

inspector.rationale: These central consumers remain downstream of publish-only telemetry and outside the actuation control path.

nodes.title: AEA Return
nodes.subtitle: psM+O zone (AEA)
nodes.description: Panel B terminal representing the AEA-side recipient of non-plant-specific VoR status returns.

inspector.rationale: PB_AEA makes the Panel B status-return endpoint explicit so PB_ACK terminates at the AEA side rather than looping back into Step 1.

nodes.title: Rejection Output
nodes.subtitle: Acceptance failure branch
nodes.description: Panel B terminal for the explicit rejection branch emitted by the acceptance stage.

inspector.rationale: PB_REJECT must leave the main ribbon and terminate in a rejection output, not cycle back into Auth & Authorisation.
F_GW1 is the CPC-side subscription edge that begins the NE 177 read-only chain.
notes: This edge carries sensing only and does not imply any write-back capability.
constraints: Source must remain A2.

inspector.rationale: F_GW2 moves telemetry from retrieval into the diode stage of the gateway chain.
notes: Rendered with diode symbolism to reinforce the one-way guarantee.
constraints: No return path may be implied.

edges.label: "no return path"
edges.detail: Rendered with a diode symbol to make the unidirectional guarantee visually explicit.

inspector.rationale: F_GW3 explicitly encodes the NE 177 'no return path' requirement and is the core visual proof for claim C2.
notes: This is the data-diode guarantee, not a general gateway transfer arrow.
constraints: Must remain solid and diode-marked.

inspector.rationale: F1 is the primary telemetry ingress into the AEA and the only solid read arrow crossing from the gateway into Lane B.
constraints: Must remain read-only.
Must enter S1, not S2.

inspector.rationale: F2 converts the read-only OPC UA stream into the harmonized PA-DIM-aligned telemetry snapshot used downstream.
constraints: Keeps sensing inside the Sense band.

inspector.rationale: F3a now terminates at the Retrieval Guard so AAS submodels pass through provenance and scope checks before the RAG stage.
constraints: Source must remain DEC_K1.

inspector.rationale: F3b provides soft-grounding policy context, but only after the Retrieval Guard checks provenance, scope, and trust classification.
notes: This edge must be clearly distinguished from F3b', which is hard enforcement.
constraints: Soft grounding only.

inspector.rationale: F_R0_out carries only ACL-approved, provenance-tagged chunks into the RAG stage.
constraints: Content must carry provenance metadata before reaching DEC_R1.

inspector.rationale: F3b' is the authoritative hard-enforcement path feeding deterministic policy rules directly into the Policy-Guard.
notes: Without F3b' the guard would have no non-bypassable policy source.
constraints: Must remain visually heavier than F3b.
Hard enforcement / non-bypassable.

inspector.rationale: F3c now terminates at the Input / Context Guard so retrieved context cannot bypass deterministic classification before reaching the planner.

edges.label: harmonised plant state (snapshot t0) to guarded ingress

inspector.rationale: F3d now terminates at DEC_G0 so the harmonised t0 plant-state snapshot is deterministically classified before model access.
constraints: Must share the same t0 snapshot group as F3h.

inspector.rationale: F_G0_pol feeds refusal and sensitive-topic rules directly into DEC_G0 so refusal logic remains external to the model.
constraints: Refusal rules must be versioned external policy.

inspector.rationale: F_G0_out is the sole planner-context ingress after deterministic classification, redaction, refusal, and trust assignment.
constraints: No contextual data may reach DEC_R2 without traversing DEC_G0.

inspector.rationale: F3e now terminates at the Output Guard / Schema Checker so raw model output cannot reach downstream deterministic gates unchecked.
F_G1A_pass carries only schema-conformant plan objects to the Policy-Guard.
constraints: Only structural plan fields may traverse this edge.

inspector.rationale: F_G1A_reject returns machine-readable schema failures to the planner without exposing downstream deterministic logic as free text.
constraints: Reject payloads must be machine-readable and bounded.

inspector.rationale: Only policy-compliant candidates reach the deterministic validators, making F3f the handoff into hard validation.
F3f_reject closes the ReAct loop so the planner can observe policy rejections and revise its proposal.
constraints: Must remain dashed and visually below F3e.

inspector.rationale: F3g supplies AAS-typed property bounds, units, and constraints to deterministic validation.
notes: Explains AAS bounds and typed property constraints.
constraints: Source must remain DEC_K1.

inspector.rationale: F3h provides the same frozen t0 values used by F3d so validation and reasoning observe the same plant snapshot.
notes: Shares the same t0 snapshot group as F3d.
constraints: Must remain synchronized with F3d.

edges.label: permitted write scope / OPC UA session roles

inspector.rationale: F3i originates at the VoR Interface because the gateway-side enforcement point owns the permitted write scope and session role knowledge.
notes: This corrected source prevents the architecture from implying client-side self-asserted authorization.
constraints: Source must remain VOI.

inspector.rationale: F_G2_reject returns deterministic validator failures to the planner so bounds, scope, or stale-snapshot violations are observable and revisable.
notes: This loop-back also carries failures that surface after F_H1_revalidate sends a stale approval back through DEC_G2.
constraints: Reject payloads must stay machine-readable.
Must remain planner-visible.

inspector.rationale: F_T1 is now dispatched only by the Tool Guard / Broker so read-only guarantees and request schemas are enforced before the OPC UA client is queried.
constraints: Tool calls are dotted, never dashed.

inspector.rationale: F_T2 is now dispatched only by the Tool Guard / Broker so targeted AAS lookups also remain behind deterministic request validation.
constraints: Tool calls are dotted, never dashed.

inspector.rationale: F_T0_req is the only path through which the planner can request external tools.
constraints: DEC_R2 must not source F_T1 or F_T2 directly.

inspector.rationale: F_T0_obs forces tool observations back through the same deterministic context gate as retrieved and sensed data.
constraints: Tool observations may not re-enter the planner directly.

inspector.rationale: F4 now terminates at the Human Approval Gate so validator-passed plans still require explicit operator approval before VoR composition.
F_H1_revalidate returns an approval-window failure to the deterministic validators so freshness and snapshot constraints are re-checked before any writeback can proceed.
constraints: Applies only to expired-approval or stale-snapshot outcomes.

inspector.rationale: F_H1_reject routes an explicit human rejection back to the planner so the proposal can be revised without implying any execution path.
constraints: Operator rejection must not bypass the replanning loop.

edges.label: approved plan bound to plan_hash + snapshot_id + expires_at

inspector.rationale: F_H1_pass is the only validation edge that may enter ACT1; approval is bound to a specific plan hash, snapshot, and expiry window.
constraints: F_H1_pass is the only validation edge allowed to target ACT1.

inspector.rationale: F_M1_G0 emits classification, redaction, refusal, and snapshot-consistency events from the Input / Context Guard.
F_M1_R0 emits provenance, ACL, and denied-source events from the Retrieval Guard.
F_M1_T0 emits allowlist, schema, rate-limit, and read-only enforcement events from the Tool Guard / Broker.
F_M1_G1A emits schema-pass and schema-reject events from the Output Guard / Schema Checker.
F_M1_H1 emits approve, reject, expiry, and stale-snapshot events from the Human Approval Gate.
F_M1_out forwards the aggregated guardrail event stream to the audit sink alongside ACT1's actuation record.
F_KPI shows that telemetry publication derives from harmonized plant signals, not from actuation outcomes.
constraints: KPI publication must remain decoupled from the VoR path.

inspector.rationale: F_AUDIT replaces the incorrect F_OPT idea and keeps actuation traceability separate from KPI publishing.
F5 is the exclusive write-back initiation path and therefore the first bold cross-boundary edge in the figure.
notes: Exclusive write-back initiation path.
constraints: Must remain bold.
Must route from ACT1 to VOI only.

inspector.rationale: F6 carries the mapped and verified instruction into the CPC Change Handler, not into the read-only CPC Data Interface, and remains bold as the second write-path segment.
notes: Non-plant-specific status is not carried here; only the change instruction is.
constraints: Target must remain A3.
Implemented as solid bold, not dashed.

inspector.rationale: F_VoR_ACK returns a limited, non-plant-specific status to the request composer without disclosing CPC internals.
notes: Non-plant-specific status semantics only.
constraints: Must remain dashed and below F5.

inspector.rationale: F_CPC_INT is the optional internal CPC execution edge showing how the change handler hands the verified instruction into the CPC system.
constraints: Optional within-lane edge.

inspector.rationale: F7a is the publish-only northbound KPI edge leaving the AEA and entering the central MQTT broker.
F7b carries the subscribed KPI stream from the broker into central analytics and historians.
F7_sub is the optional subscription-semantic edge and must remain dotted so it is not mistaken for status feedback.
PB_F1 moves the VoR flow from authentication into semantic verification.
PB_F2 advances the VoR pipeline into mapping.
PB_F3 advances the mapped request into the acceptance stage.
PB_F4 carries accepted requests into mapping verification and execution.

edges.label: status: accepted | rejected | executed | timeout
edges.detail: non-plant-specific; no CPC addresses or asset IDs disclosed

inspector.rationale: PB_ACK is the Panel B counterpart of F_VoR_ACK and returns limited execution status to the AEA side.
PB_REJECT shows the explicit rejection branch from acceptance back toward the request composer.

steps.title: Auth & Authorisation
steps.summary: Verify the AEA identity certificate and check write-scope against the permitted session role.
notes: Links to F3i and the gateway-assigned write scope.

steps.title: Semantic Verification
steps.summary: Validate the request payload against AAS property types, unit dimensions, and semantic identifiers.
notes: Uses AAS-typed property bounds and the shared t0 snapshot.

steps.title: Mapping
steps.summary: Translate the abstract non-plant-specific request into concrete DCS or PLC addresses and asset references.
notes: Crosses conceptually into CPC territory before acceptance.

steps.title: Acceptance
steps.summary: Evaluate whether the mapped request is accepted by the configured human or automated plausibility gate on the NE 178 / gateway side.
notes: If any endpoint cannot accept the request, the entire transaction is rejected atomically.
AEA-side pre-VoR approval remains in Panel A and the guardrail interaction view; Panel B stays NE 178-pure.

steps.title: Mapping Verification + Execution
steps.summary: Verify the mapped instruction one final time, execute atomically, and generate status plus audit records.
notes: PB_ACK corresponds directly to F_VoR_ACK in Panel A.
